Docs | Blog | Community

Understanding TypeScript Generics

Generics are one of TypeScript's most powerful features. They allow you to write reusable, type-safe code without sacrificing flexibility.

At its core, a generic is a type parameter that can be specified when a function, class, or interface is used. This lets the same implementation work with multiple types.

Consider a simple identity function. Without generics, you might use any, losing type information. With generics, the return type matches the argument type exactly.
